baseline_plan.definition: Packets move randomly over the abstract graph without local constraint, global constraint, memory ledger, causal mass, or three-time layering.
baseline_plan.name: random_walk_baseline

baseline_plan.definition: Packets are filtered only by node-local abstract constraints without global constraint, memory ledger, or causal mass.
baseline_plan.name: local_constraint_only_baseline

baseline_plan.definition: Packets use local and global constraints but do not retain prior transition or violation history.
baseline_plan.name: no_memory_baseline

baseline_plan.definition: Packets use one abstract time layer instead of the t1, t2, t3 representation.
baseline_plan.name: single_time_layer_baseline

baseline_plan.definition: Packets use constraints and memory but exclude delayed causal-mass scoring.
baseline_plan.name: no_causal_mass_baseline

falsification_rules.name: baseline_non_difference_rule
falsification_rules.rule: If Viruse Fabric outputs do not differ from all relevant baselines under predeclared metrics, the corresponding hypothesis is not supported.

falsification_rules.name: ledger_no_effect_rule
falsification_rules.rule: If memory-ledger variants do not reduce violations or change stability compared with no-memory baselines, the ledger-stability claim is not supported.

falsification_rules.name: causal_mass_no_delay_rule
falsification_rules.rule: If causal-mass variants do not generate delayed-effect differences, the causal-mass delayed-effect claim is not supported.

falsification_rules.name: three_time_no_projection_rule
falsification_rules.rule: If t1/t2/t3 variants do not differ from single-time variants, the three-time-layer claim is not supported.

falsification_rules.name: seed_instability_rule
falsification_rules.rule: If observed effects collapse across controlled random seeds, the result is treated as unstable and not validated.

falsification_rules.name: no_validation_without_execution_rule
falsification_rules.rule: No theory validation is claimed until v9.2 engine execution, v9.3 baseline comparison, and v9.4 falsification audit are completed.

metric_proposals.definition: Fraction of toy pattern packets remaining active after a fixed number of abstract steps.
metric_proposals.name: survival_rate

metric_proposals.definition: Diversity of packet positions over the abstract graph.
metric_proposals.name: spread_entropy

metric_proposals.definition: Fraction of transitions that violate declared abstract constraints.
metric_proposals.name: constraint_violation_rate

metric_proposals.definition: Degree to which packet paths remain consistent under controlled random seeds.
metric_proposals.name: path_stability

metric_proposals.definition: Difference between immediate transition outcomes and later t2 or t3 outcomes.
metric_proposals.name: delayed_effect_score

metric_proposals.definition: Distance between Viruse Fabric model outputs and baseline outputs under predeclared toy metrics.
metric_proposals.name: baseline_divergence

metric_proposals.definition: Rate of unitless symbolic packet-state perturbation over toy steps.
metric_proposals.name: symbolic_drift_rate

metric_proposals.definition: Difference between memory-ledger and no-memory model outputs.
metric_proposals.name: ledger_effect_size

model_objects.definition: A finite directed or undirected toy graph whose nodes represent abstract environments and whose edges represent abstract transition channels.
model_objects.name: abstract_environment_graph
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: No real location, host, organism, receptor, pathogen, tissue, or biological substrate is represented.

model_objects.definition: A symbolic toy agent carrying abstract state variables such as compatibility, cost, memory trace, and constraint score.
model_objects.name: pattern_packet
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: The packet is not a biological agent, pathogen, molecule, cell, genome, protein, receptor, or wet-lab object.

model_objects.definition: A unitless rule layer applied at individual nodes to determine whether a pattern packet remains, moves, degrades, or is filtered.
model_objects.name: local_constraint_field
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: The field is mathematical only and contains no real biological parameters.

model_objects.definition: A unitless rule layer that applies cross-node pressure or system-level restrictions over the toy graph.
model_objects.name: global_constraint_field
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: The field does not model real-world biosafety, infectivity, tropism, or host range.

model_objects.definition: A unitless aggregate influence score intended to represent delayed or distributed abstract causal pressure.
model_objects.name: causal_mass
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: Causal mass is not a physical, biological, clinical, or epidemiological measurement.

model_objects.definition: A state history layer that stores prior abstract decisions, violations, transitions, and delayed effects.
model_objects.name: memory_ledger
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: The ledger stores toy simulation state only and does not store real biological or personal data.

model_objects.definition: A tri-temporal representation using t1, t2, and t3 to separate current state, delayed effect, and projected constraint state.
model_objects.name: three_time_layer_state
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: The time layers are abstract simulation indices, not real clinical or biological timelines.

model_objects.definition: A purely symbolic perturbation operator that changes unitless toy attributes of a pattern packet.
model_objects.name: symbolic_mutation_operator
model_objects.non_operational_boundary: This operator is not genetic, biological, molecular, phenotypic, immune, host-range, or infectivity optimization.

non_validation_disclaimer: Abstract simulation reframing only. No simulation engine is created. No simulation run is performed. No baseline comparison is executed. No results are reported. No falsification audit is executed. No validation claim is made. No manuscript file is modified. No citation is added. No real biological datasets, no real pathogen models, no receptor parameters, and no operational targeting are introduced.

reframed_hypotheses.falsification_condition: If transition distributions are not detectably different from local-only baseline under predeclared toy metrics, H1 is not supported.
reframed_hypotheses.hypothesis: In an abstract graph, combining local and global constraints changes packet transition paths compared with a local-only baseline.
reframed_hypotheses.name: multi_layer_constraint_path_shift

reframed_hypotheses.falsification_condition: If violation rates and stability metrics do not improve over the no-memory baseline, H2 is not supported.
reframed_hypotheses.hypothesis: A memory-ledger layer reduces repeated constraint violations and increases path stability compared with a no-memory baseline.
reframed_hypotheses.name: memory_ledger_stability_effect

reframed_hypotheses.falsification_condition: If delayed-effect metrics are indistinguishable from the random-walk baseline, H3 is not supported.
reframed_hypotheses.hypothesis: A causal-mass term produces delayed transition effects that are absent in a memory-free random-walk baseline.
reframed_hypotheses.name: causal_mass_delayed_effect

reframed_hypotheses.falsification_condition: If projected constraint outcomes do not differ from a single-time-layer baseline, H4 is not supported.
reframed_hypotheses.hypothesis: A three-time-layer state representation produces different projected constraint outcomes than a single-time-layer state representation.
reframed_hypotheses.name: three_time_layer_predictive_difference

roadmap.name: Viruse Fabric Abstract Simulation Reframing
roadmap.purpose: Define theory as a formal, simulatable, falsifiable abstract toy model.

roadmap.name: Abstract Simulation Specification
roadmap.purpose: Specify exact graph generation, update rules, metrics, seeds, and output schema.

roadmap.name: Minimal Safe Toy Simulation Engine
roadmap.purpose: Implement the first non-operational abstract toy simulation engine.

roadmap.name: Baseline Comparison
roadmap.purpose: Compare Viruse Fabric variants against predeclared baselines.

roadmap.name: Results and Falsification Audit
roadmap.purpose: Report results and explicitly identify supported, unsupported, or inconclusive hypotheses.

safety_boundaries.counter_requirement: Real biological dataset import count remains zero.
Real pathogen simulation count remains zero.
Real receptor parameter count remains zero.
Operational host targeting count remains zero.
Actionable biosafety-risk instruction count remains zero.
Real-world infectivity optimization count remains zero.
Immune evasion optimization count remains zero.
Real host range prediction count remains zero.

safety_boundaries.boundary: No validation, readiness, citation integration, or manuscript mutation in v9.0.
safety_boundaries.counter_requirement: Validation, readiness, citation, and manuscript mutation counters remain zero.

state_variables.definition: Toy environment state attached to each graph node.
state_variables.name: node_state

state_variables.definition: Toy symbolic attributes carried by each pattern packet.
state_variables.name: packet_state

state_variables.definition: Toy match score between packet state and node state.
state_variables.name: compatibility_score

state_variables.definition: Prior transition and constraint history retained for path dependence.
state_variables.name: memory_trace

state_variables.definition: Magnitude of symbolic toy-state perturbation over time.
state_variables.name: symbolic_drift

state_variables.definition: Whether a packet remains active in the toy system.
state_variables.name: survival_indicator

state_variables.definition: Toy action selected at a step: remain, move, filtered, or symbolic perturbation.
state_variables.name: transition_choice

state_variables.definition: Whether a packet violates a declared abstract constraint.
state_variables.name: violation_indicator

state_variables.definition: One of t1, t2, or t3 abstract simulation layers.
state_variables.name: time_layer

theory_reframing_statement: Viruse Fabric is reframed as a safe abstract toy model over graph-based symbolic pattern packets, local and global constraints, causal-mass scoring, memory-ledger path dependence, and t1/t2/t3 time-layered state. This v9.0 milestone defines a formal, simulatable, and falsifiable theory frame without creating a simulation engine and without executing any simulation.
title: Viruse Fabric Abstract Simulation Reframing
